provenance.reconstruction_notes: Reconstructed from memory/2026-05-16.md Infrastructure & Systems, memory/2026-05-19.md Infrastructure & Systems / Daily Reflection entries, chronic-issues.json#disk-cleanup-threshold, and scripts/disk-cleanup.sh comments. The checklist date names the prior scope-fix day; the verified false-success recurrence closed on 2026-05-19 when the cleaner reported zero reclaimed bytes for six runs while disk rose and large /tmp project clones stayed below the time-band threshold. Internal hostnames, IPs, and private operator identifiers are intentionally omitted.

snapshots.summary: The May16 cleanup run reported success and large reclaimed space after adding /tmp project cleanup.
May19 live inspection found local disk had risen while cleanup summaries still reported zero reclaimed bytes.
Largest-directory inspection found two multi-GB /tmp project clones below the 72h band threshold.

payload.selector: prior cleanup scope and age-band rules
payload.result_summary: Loaded the newly broadened cleanup policy: user-owned /tmp project clones with source/build markers, minimum size, active-file skip, and age bands based on disk pressure.

payload.query: run cleanup after adding guarded /tmp project cleanup
payload.result_summary: Cleaner removed stale /tmp project clones and dropped disk usage from the low seventies to the high fifties, creating a reasonable but overconfident belief that the scope issue was fixed.

payload.summary: Recorded the May16 /tmp cleanup as fix-applied with 72h retention in the 70-85% disk band and no size override for fast-growing clones.

payload.query: compare disk trend against recent cleanup summaries
payload.result_summary: Disk rose from 69% to 79% over about 36h while six cleanup runs reported zero MB reclaimed, so green cleanup status no longer matched retained-artifact health.

payload.query: identify retained scratch directories and active-file state
payload.result_summary: Inspection found roughly 34GB of user-owned /tmp data, led by one 17.9GB project clone at 51h old and one 7.1GB clone at 25h old; active-file checks found no open files.

payload.intent: Explain why apparently stale multi-GB /tmp project clones survived successful cleanup runs.
payload.result_summary: The 70-85% disk band required 72h age before deletion. Large contribution clones can now exceed 5GB within 12-51h, so the cleaner correctly followed the spec while the spec silently failed the operational need.

payload.summary: Tightened /tmp project cleanup: 70-85% disk band now uses 24h retention, >=85% uses 12h, and any qualifying clone >=5GB and >=12h old is cleaned despite the band threshold.

payload.query: verify revised cleanup policy on retained /tmp project clones
payload.result_summary: Live run cleaned five qualifying project directories, reclaimed 25,912 MB, and reduced disk usage from 79% to 58%.

payload.summary: Persisted the tighter thresholds and chronic-issue note: the prior fix solved stale-scope cleanup but missed fast-growing large clones below the age gate.
Cleanup status looked green because the script followed its own stale age-band spec; retained-artifact inspection showed the spec no longer matched contribution-build behavior.
payload.label: false_completion
